violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee(s) must register in, and successfully complete, an approved course in food safety management. An employee certified in food safety management must be on-site at all times of operation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Thermometers are lacking inside the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

The cutting boards at the deli meat section of the service line are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. An on-site employee certified in food safety management must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

Thermometers are lacking inside the refrigeration units (walk-in cooler and 2-door lowboy unit behind counter). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2025

The 2-door lowboy unit behind the counter is in poor repair. It is not in use at the time of this inspection. This unit must be properly repaired to maintain a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. Corrective action date: 05/05/2025. In addition, the thresholds of the walk-in cooler and the walk-in freezer are in poor repair, being held in place by tape. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. The thresholds must be properly repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. An on-site employee certified in food safety management must apply with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

Walk-in cooler was observed held at 57 degrees Fahrenheit. Product in this unit observed with temperatures of 55 - 57 degrees F. In addition, the 2-door lowboy unit behind the counter observed with an ambient temperature of 43 degrees F. Product observed at 43 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Service had already been scheduled for today. Voluntary disposal of foods in the walk-in cooler during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2025

Thermometers are lacking inside the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2025

The thresholds of the walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer are in poor repair, being held in place by tape. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. Walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer thresholds are to be properly repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
